hero.label: Made for 14.ai
hero.headline: I want to help 14.ai hit $10M ARR.
hero.subheadline: You're building the first AI-native customer service agency for DTC brands, and you just got featured in TechCrunch. I spent a year doing full-cycle outbound at an AI startup, and I know how to find, qualify, and close the brands who need this.

whyCompany.title: AI-native BPOs are replacing entire support teams
whyCompany.body: 14.ai isn't selling a chatbot. You're replacing entire BPO contracts with AI agents that reply in under 10 seconds. For DTC brands spending $50K-$500K/year on outsourced support, the ROI pitch writes itself. I want to be the one making that pitch.

whyCompany.title: 3 people, YC W24, backed by Vercel and Slack founders
whyCompany.body: This is as early-stage and high-upside as it gets. Founding GTM at a 3-person company means I'm not following a playbook. I'm writing it. The 1% equity tells me you're serious about this being a career-defining hire, not just another SDR seat.

whyCompany.title: My outbound experience maps perfectly to DTC sales
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I prospected, qualified, and helped close 15-20 B2B SaaS deals from scratch. At NextAfter, I learned how digital marketing and fundraising strategies drive revenue at a consultancy. Selling AI customer service to Shopify and TikTok Shop brands? That's the intersection of everything I've done.

quote: Three people, one massive market, and a product TechCrunch just wrote about. I don't need a playbook. I need a phone and a target list.
